text: In elixir phoenix project, how to add dont icon? # Adding Font Icons to a Phoenix Project To add font icons (like Font Awesome, Material Icons, etc.) to your Phoenix project, you have several options: ## 1. Using NPM/Yarn (Recommended for Phoenix 1.6+ with esbuild) If your Phoenix project uses esbuild: 1. Install the font icon package: ```bash # Example for Font Awesome cd assets npm install @fortawesome/fontawesome-free # or yarn add @fortawesome/fontawesome-free ``` 2. Import the CSS in your `app.js` file: ```javascript // In assets/js/app.js import "@fortawesome/fontawesome-free/css/all.min.css" ``` ## 2. Using CDN (Simple approach) Add the font icon CDN link to your layout file: ```html ``` ## 3. Direct Download (Manual approach) 1. Download the font icon package 2. Extract files to `priv/static/fonts` and `priv/static/css` 3. Reference in your HTML layout ## Using the Icons Once added, you can use icons in your templates: ```html home ``` ## Popular Font Icon Libraries - Font Awesome: [fontawesome.com](https://fontawesome.com/) - Material Icons: [material.io/icons](https://material.io/icons) - Heroicons: [heroicons.com](https://heroicons.com/) - Bootstrap Icons: [icons.getbootstrap.com](https://icons.getbootstrap.com/) Choose the library that best fits your design needs.
summary: Adding Font Icons to Phoenix Project
